v2.heroSubtitle: Simulate Arduino, ESP32, Raspberry Pi Pico, and Raspberry Pi 3 in your browser. 19 boards, 68+ ready-to-run examples, realistic sensor simulation. Free, open-source, no install needed.
v2.boardsLead: From 8-bit AVR to quad-core ARM Linux — Velxio 2.0 emulates every major embedded platform in a single browser tab.
v2.mixArchitecturesBody: Connect an ESP32 to a Raspberry Pi 3, wire three Arduinos together, or combine a Pico with an ESP32-C3. Any combination works.
v2.realInterBoardBody: Boards communicate through wired connections — UART, I2C, SPI, or simple GPIO signals, just like real hardware.
v2.uniqueFeatureBody: Traditional simulators emulate one board in isolation. Velxio simulates entire systems with multiple boards running simultaneously.
v2.whatsNewLead: A complete transformation into a multi-platform embedded systems simulator — more boards, more accurate emulation, better developer experience.
v2.examplesLead: From basic LED blink to multi-board communication — examples for every platform and skill level.

arduino.title: Arduino (17 examples)
arduino.body: Blink, traffic light, button, fade, RGB LED, Simon Says game, TFT display, LCD, I2C scanner, SPI loopback, and more.

pico.title: Raspberry Pi Pico (18 examples)
pico.body: Blink, serial, I2C (scanner, RTC, EEPROM), SPI, ADC read, DHT22, HC-SR04, servo, joystick, NTC sensor.

esp32.title: ESP32 (9 examples)
esp32.body: Blink, serial echo, 7-segment, DHT22, HC-SR04, MPU6050 IMU, PIR motion, servo, joystick.

esp32c3.title: ESP32-C3 RISC-V (9 examples)
esp32c3.body: Blink, serial, RGB LED, button + LED, serial echo, DHT22, HC-SR04, PIR, servo.

mega.title: Arduino Mega (5 examples)
mega.body: Blink, LED chase, serial echo, serial control, 7-segment display.

nano.title: Arduino Nano (4 examples)
nano.body: Blink, button LED, fade, serial echo. Plus multi-board Pi-to-Arduino control.

v2.outcomeLead: Velxio 2.0 transforms the project into a complete, scalable multi-platform embedded systems simulator.

outcome.realisticBody: QEMU-based Xtensa, RISC-V and ARM emulation. Cycle-accurate AVR8 via avr8js. Real firmware execution, not approximations.
outcome.broaderBody: 19 boards across 5 CPU architectures. From 8-bit ATtiny85 to quad-core Raspberry Pi 3B running full Linux.
outcome.devExBody: Monaco editor, multi-file workspace, library manager, oscilloscope, sensor control panel, mobile-responsive design.

v2.builtOnOssLead: Velxio is powered by these open-source projects. Without them, none of this would be possible.

bottom.title: Try Velxio 2.0 now
bottom.body: Open the editor and start simulating — 19 boards, 68+ examples, zero setup.

v25.heroAccent: Arduino meets SPICE in your browser
v25.heroSubtitle: Real-time analog circuit simulation via ngspice-WASM, wired to Arduino, ESP32, and Raspberry Pi Pico. Resistors, capacitors, op-amps, transistors, live ammeters and voltmeters — co-simulated with your firmware. Free, open-source, no install.
v25.fullEmulation: From digital-only to full circuit emulation
v25.fullEmulationLead: Velxio 2.0 simulated your firmware perfectly. Velxio 2.5 simulates the circuit around it — with the same ngspice engine used by professional EDA tools, running entirely in your browser tab.
v25.notLinearBody: Velxio solves the full Modified Nodal Analysis every tick. Non-linear devices — diodes, BJTs, MOSFETs, op-amps with saturation — behave like the real thing, not like textbook idealisations.
v25.realFirmware: Real firmware. Real circuit. Same canvas.
v25.realFirmwareBody: GPIO pins drive SPICE nets as voltage sources. ADC inputs read solved node voltages. analogRead() returns what the circuit actually produces — not a simulated shortcut.
v25.browserOfflineBody: ngspice compiled to WebAssembly via the open-source eecircuit-engine. No server, no cloud, no account. Works offline after the first load. Your circuits never leave your machine.
v25.catalogLead: A complete electronics catalogue — passives, semiconductors, logic, instruments — every one of them modelled with real ngspice device cards, not behavioural stand-ins.
v25.coSimLead: Most browser simulators run either your firmware OR a circuit — never both together. Velxio 2.5 solves them on the same clock.
v25.driveMotor: Drive a motor from an Arduino pin
v25.driveMotorBody: Pin HIGH → MOSFET gate → drain current through a real inductive load → back-EMF through a flyback diode. All SPICE-solved. Scope the gate and drain voltages side by side.
v25.readSensor: Read a sensor through a real op-amp
v25.readSensorBody: Photodiode → transimpedance op-amp → ADC. Adjust the lux slider and watch analogRead() respond through the actual amplifier stage — saturation, slew rate, and all.
v25.liveInstrumentsBody: Drop an ammeter between any two nodes, a voltmeter on any net. The oscilloscope captures digital pins and analog nodes together. No "what if" — just measurements.
v25.examplesLead: Pre-wired circuits you can open in one click — from textbook fundamentals to realistic Arduino+analog systems.

fundamentals.title: Fundamentals
fundamentals.body: Voltage divider, RC low-pass filter, RC high-pass filter, LR circuit, capacitor charging, RLC resonance, Wheatstone bridge.

diodes.title: Diodes & rectifiers
diodes.body: Half-wave rectifier, full-wave bridge rectifier, Zener regulator, clamper, clipper, voltage doubler.

opamps.title: Op-amp circuits
opamps.body: Inverting amplifier, non-inverting amplifier, summing amp, differential amp, integrator, differentiator, Schmitt trigger, comparator.

transistors.title: Transistor circuits
transistors.body: Common-emitter amplifier, transistor switch, Darlington pair, MOSFET driver, H-bridge, current mirror.

arduino.title: Arduino + analog
arduino.body: PWM-driven LED with RC smoothing, Arduino-controlled MOSFET motor driver, opto-isolated relay, photodiode + op-amp + ADC pipeline.

sensors.title: Sensors
sensors.body: NTC thermistor bridge, photoresistor light meter, photodiode transimpedance, potentiometer dimmer, flame detector.

v25.outcomeLead: Velxio 2.5 closes the loop between firmware and hardware: the same tool that runs your sketch now solves the circuit around it.

outcome.teachBody: A free, browser-based SPICE playground for students — with the added benefit of driving real microcontroller firmware. No license, no install, no lab booking.
outcome.prototypeBody: Validate analog front-ends, power stages, and sensor pipelines end-to-end before you order a single part. The scope and DMM are already on the bench.
outcome.openSourceBody: AGPL-3.0 on GitHub. Built on ngspice, eecircuit-engine, avr8js, rp2040js, QEMU, and wokwi-elements. Fork it, host it, extend it.

v25.builtOnOssLead: Velxio 2.5 stands on the shoulders of decades of electronics tooling. Huge thanks to the maintainers of every project below.

bottom.title: Try Velxio 2.5 now
bottom.body: Open the editor and start simulating — firmware and analog circuit, same canvas, zero setup.
